title: Fixing CSS Inconsistent Box Model Styles

statement.markdown: The task is to fix a function that calculates the total height of an HTML element with a given width, padding, and margin. The function should return the total height as a string in the format 'total height: <total_height>px'. The function should work for both block-level and inline-block elements. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that causes incorrect results for certain input values. Fix the bug to make the function work correctly for all cases.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be in the format 'width: <width>px, padding: <padding>px, margin: <margin>px'.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be in the format 'total height: <total_height>px'.
statement.notes: The function should work for both block-level and inline-block elements. The total height should include the height of the element, as well as the top and bottom padding and margin.

examples.explanation: The total height should include the height of the element (100px), as well as the top and bottom padding (10px) and margin (20px).
The total height should include the height of the element (500px), as well as the top and bottom padding (50px) and margin (0px).

hints.text: Remember to check for both block-level and inline-block elements.
Make sure to include the height of the element in the total height.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Inconsistent Box Model Styles
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to correctly calculate the total height of the element, including the height of the element, as well as the top and bottom padding and margin. The function should work for both block-level and inline-block elements.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach to solving this problem is to write a separate function for each type of element and use a large number of if-else statements to handle the different cases. However, this approach is inefficient and prone to errors.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use a single function that takes into account the type of element and calculates the total height accordingly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a single function that uses the following formula to calculate the total height: total_height = height + 2 * padding + 2 * margin. This formula works for both block-level and inline-block elements and correctly handles the different cases.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the formula used in the optimal solution correctly calculates the total height of the element, including the height of the element, as well as the top and bottom padding and margin.
editorial.dryRun: Here is a step-by-step example of how the optimal solution works: 1. Parse the input string to extract the width, padding, and margin values. 2. Calculate the total height using the formula: total_height = height + 2 * padding + 2 * margin. 3. Return the total height as a string in the format 'total height: <total_height>px'.
commonMistakes: forgetting to include the height of the element
